vulnerability.reference: Partial observability and lagged communication cause inconsistent world models, amplifying conflicting corrections.
vulnerability.generated: Shared workspace lacks real-time safety override or human oversight to intercept robots' self-corrected but misaligned plans.

impact.reference: Robot collisions leading to costly downtime and damaged inventory.
impact.generated: Collisions between robots and human workers lead to worker injuries, damaged goods, and warehouse operation disruptions.

dose_response.reference: LLM-driven planning with self-correction amplifies erroneous abstractions, which, in partially observable warehouses with lagged communication, leads to conflicting robot actions exposing workers to collisions and inventory damage.
dose_response.generated: LLM-driven planning with iterative self-correction amplifies misaligned objectives and erroneous abstractions, through shared human-robot spaces, triggering the lack of real-time safety override and human oversight, leading to collisions causing worker injuries, damaged goods, and disruptions.
